user_task: Agentic retrieval - multi-turn (query rewrite and multi-turn).
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, RETRIEVE bucket -> 'Agentic retrieval - multi-turn (query rewrite and multi-turn)'; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever - JTBD seed queries' tab, RETRIEVE -> agentic multi-turn (Turn 1 'What does the table show for James?' -> Turn 2 'And Susan?' -> Turn 3 'Which of them scored higher in 2019?'; plus a vague query the agent rewrites into a precise retriever query).

success_criteria.quality: operational pass (NOT RAGAS): each conversational turn is resolved by the AGENT into a well-formed STANDALONE `retriever query` (pronouns/ellipsis/comparatives rewritten using prior-turn context), the right subcommand+flags are used, and the returned grounded hit yields the correct answer. Binary pass/fail per turn. The retriever is stateless per call; the agent owns the conversation state.
success_criteria.time: agentic RETRIEVE -> <= 5 min per case (note: a longer budget than other RETRIEVE rows, which are <= 1 min, because each case is a multi-call conversation: one ingest + a SEQUENCE of standalone queries, one per turn).
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% - a 'have a multi-turn conversation over this table / answer this follow-up' prompt must fire the skill, and must fire `retriever query` again for each follow-up turn
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% - each turn must run `retriever query "<rewritten standalone q>"` (with --content-types text,table to hit the table cell after a `ingest --use-table-structure`); no --input-type flag (does not exist); no attempt to pass conversation history / session id to the CLI (the CLI has no such flag)

environment.format_autodetect: `retriever ingest <file>` auto-detects PDF from the extension. There is NO --input-type flag.
environment.statelessness_caveat: NRL 26.05 has NO conversational/session state. There is no `--history`, `--session`, `--context`, or `--turn` flag on `retriever query`; the CLI takes exactly one query string and returns hits for THAT string only. This maps to the spec's negative-test category (d): a bare elliptical follow-up like 'What about the Q3 numbers?' carries NO multi-turn context inside NRL. Therefore multi-turn + query rewrite MUST be implemented at the AGENT layer: the agent rewrites each user turn into a self-contained query that names the entity/year/comparison explicitly, then issues a fresh stateless `retriever query`. These tests verify the AGENT rewrites/resolves references across turns and issues correct standalone queries - NOT that NRL maintains a session.
environment.ingest_for_this_suite: Table values live in a TABLE, so ingest with --use-table-structure (nemotron-table-structure-v1) so cells are queryable as content_type=table rows; query with --content-types text,table. Ingest once per case; the conversation is a sequence of queries against the same table.

extraction_defaults_from_dry_run.note: --use-table-structure flips use_table_structure true and table_output_format to markdown so James/Susan year cells are recoverable as discrete content_type=table rows rather than flattened text.

environment.ingest_success_line: Ingested N file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/<table>.
environment.query_hit_shape: JSON array; each hit has exactly {source, page_number, text}; page_number is a 1-indexed int.

fixture_ground_truth.table_test.pdf: p1 table Year x {Bill,Amy,James,Ted,Susan}. James 2019 = 978; Susan 2019 = 922 (James > Susan in 2019). James 2023 = 539; Susan 2023 = 970. James 2020 = 922; Susan 2020 = 994. Susan 2018 = 976.
fixture_ground_truth.woods_frost.pdf: p1 Robert Frost poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening' (owner's 'house is in the village', 'miles to go before I sleep'); p2 table of Frost collections x years incl. 'New Hampshire - 1923'.

cases.title: Single vague turn -> agent REWRITES it into a precise standalone retriever query
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the QUERY-REWRITE half of the task in its simplest, single-turn form: the user gives one vague/underspecified utterance and the agent must rewrite it into a well-formed retriever query that returns the right table cell. Rung-1 floor - NO prior-turn context to carry yet (a single turn), so this isolates rewrite from multi-turn. Adds nothing over a prior rung (it is the floor).
cases.prompt: Here is data/table_test.pdf. I'm looking at James's row - what's his number for the 2019 line?
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "James value in 2019 from the table" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (table rows present)
expected_output.rewrite: vague 'what's his number for the 2019 line?' -> standalone retriever query naming the entity (James) + year (2019) explicitly, e.g. 'James value in 2019 from the table'
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}; >= 1 table-derived hit citing table_test.pdf p1

pass_when: agent ingests table_test.pdf with --use-table-structure so cells are content_type=table rows
agent REWRITES the vague utterance into a standalone query that explicitly names James AND 2019 (does not pass the raw vague text verbatim, and does not rely on any CLI session state)
query uses --content-types text,table and returns a table-derived hit
answer is exactly 978 citing table_test.pdf p1

cases.title: Two-turn: elliptical follow-up ('and Susan?') resolved using turn-1 context
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the MULTI-TURN half: a two-turn exchange where turn 2 is elliptical ('And Susan?') and only resolvable from turn-1 context. Adds over rung 1 exactly one dimension: a SECOND turn whose meaning depends on the first - the agent must carry the '2019' constraint from turn 1 and rewrite the bare 'And Susan?' into a standalone query. Each turn is its own stateless `retriever query`; NRL is not told about turn 1.
cases.prompt: Use data/table_test.pdf. Turn 1: 'What does the table show for James in 2019?' Then, as a follow-up, Turn 2: 'And Susan?'
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "James value in 2019 from the table" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table
$RETRIEVER query "Susan value in 2019 from the table" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (table rows present)
expected_output.turn1: rewrite 'James in 2019' -> standalone query -> James 2019 = 978 (table_test.pdf p1)
expected_output.turn2_rewrite: the bare 'And Susan?' is rewritten using turn-1 context (the carried '2019' constraint) into a STANDALONE query 'Susan value in 2019 from the table' - the agent supplies '2019', NRL does not remember it

pass_when: two distinct `retriever query` calls are issued, one per turn (the follow-up fires the skill again)
turn 1 resolves to James 2019 = 978

turn 2: the agent CARRIES the 2019 constraint from turn 1 and rewrites the elliptical 'And Susan?' into a standalone query naming Susan AND 2019 - it does NOT issue a bare 'Susan' query and does NOT rely on CLI session memory
turn 2 answer is exactly Susan 2019 = 922 citing table_test.pdf p1
fails if 'And Susan?' is sent to NRL verbatim expecting NRL to remember '2019' (NRL is stateless)
cases.known_caveat: Negative-test category (d): NRL 26.05 has no session state. A bare 'And Susan?' (or 'What about the Q3 numbers?') passed to `retriever query` returns hits for that string alone, with NO carried context. The agent must rewrite it; this rung is built to exercise that agent-layer carry.

cases.title: Three-turn with a comparative/derived follow-up ('which scored higher in 2019?')
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the seed conversation in full (Turn 1 James -> Turn 2 Susan -> Turn 3 comparison). Adds over rung 2 exactly one dimension: a THIRD turn that is COMPARATIVE/derived ('Which of them scored higher in 2019?') - it references BOTH prior entities and requires the agent to either re-issue standalone queries for both and compute the comparison itself, or confirm the two carried values. NRL returns cells, not comparisons; the comparison is an agent-layer computation over standalone-query results.
cases.prompt: Using data/table_test.pdf, hold a 3-turn conversation. Turn 1: 'What does the table show for James in 2019?' Turn 2: 'And Susan?' Turn 3: 'Which of them scored higher that year?'
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "James value in 2019 from the table" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table
$RETRIEVER query "Susan value in 2019 from the table" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table
$RETRIEVER query "James and Susan values in 2019 from the table" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (table rows present)
expected_output.turn3_rewrite: 'Which of them scored higher that year?' is comparative + uses the carried 'that year' = 2019 and carried entities James,Susan; the agent resolves it to a standalone retrieval of both 2019 cells (or reuses the two carried values) and COMPUTES the comparison itself
expected_output.turn3_answer: James scored higher in 2019 (978 > 922); cite table_test.pdf p1. NRL returns the cells; the > comparison is agent-layer.

pass_when: three turns -> at least three `retriever query` calls (one per turn; turn 3 may re-query both entities or reuse turns 1-2 results, but must NOT rely on CLI state)
turn 1 = James 2019 = 978; turn 2 = Susan 2019 = 922
turn 3: the agent resolves the comparative 'which of them ... that year' by carrying entities (James, Susan) AND 'that year' (2019) into standalone queries, then computing the comparison
turn 3 answer: James scored higher in 2019 (978 > 922), grounded in table_test.pdf p1
the comparison is done at the AGENT layer over standalone-query results (NRL returns cells, not a 'higher' verdict)
cases.known_caveat: NRL has no aggregate/comparison operator and no session state. The agent must (a) rewrite each turn into a standalone query and (b) compute the 'higher' comparison itself from the two returned cells.

cases.title: Reference resolution: pronoun/ellipsis ('that year', 'the higher one') correctly rewritten
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the explicit 'query rewrite resolves pronouns/ellipsis' clause as its own rung, and stresses it: a chain where later turns contain a bare pronoun ('that year') AND a definite-description anaphor ('the higher one') that can only be bound from EARLIER turns. Adds over rung 3 one dimension: the follow-up's referent is the RESULT of a prior turn (the winning entity), not just a carried literal - forcing the agent to track derived state across turns. Includes a second document (woods_frost.pdf) so 'the table' itself is a reference the rewrite must disambiguate to table_test.pdf.
cases.prompt: I've loaded data/table_test.pdf and data/woods_frost.pdf. Turn 1: 'In the year 2020, what did James score in the numbers table?' Turn 2: 'Was that higher or lower than Susan that year?' Turn 3: 'Whoever scored higher that year - what did they score in 2018?'
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "James value in 2020 from the numbers table (table_test.pdf)" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table
$RETRIEVER query "Susan value in 2020 from the numbers table (table_test.pdf)" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table
$RETRIEVER query "Susan value in 2018 from the numbers table (table_test.pdf)" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 2 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (table rows from both PDFs; 'the numbers table' = table_test.pdf)
expected_output.turn1_rewrite: 'the numbers table' disambiguated to table_test.pdf (NOT woods_frost.pdf's Frost-collections table); James 2020 = 922 (table_test.pdf p1)
expected_output.turn2_rewrite: 'that year' -> 2020 (carried); rewrite to standalone 'Susan value in 2020' -> Susan 2020 = 994; agent computes 994 > 922 => James was LOWER (Susan higher in 2020)
expected_output.turn3_rewrite: 'Whoever scored higher that year' resolves to the DERIVED entity Susan (the turn-2 winner) and 'that year' = 2020; the agent then rewrites the new ask ('what did they score in 2018') to a standalone 'Susan value in 2018' -> Susan 2018 = 976
expected_output.turn3_answer: Susan (the higher scorer in 2020) scored 976 in 2018; cite table_test.pdf p1

pass_when: ingest of BOTH PDFs; the agent disambiguates 'the numbers table' to table_test.pdf in its rewrite (not woods_frost.pdf)
turn 1: James 2020 = 922 (table_test.pdf p1)
turn 2: 'that year' bound to 2020; standalone Susan-2020 query -> 994; agent computes James (922) was LOWER than Susan (994)

turn 3: the anaphor 'whoever scored higher that year' is resolved to the DERIVED winner Susan (a result of turn 2, not a literal in the prompt), and 'that year' is NOT reused (the new ask is 2018); standalone Susan-2018 query -> 976
turn 3 answer: Susan scored 976 in 2018, grounded in table_test.pdf p1
every follow-up is a fresh standalone `retriever query`; no reliance on CLI session/history
cases.known_caveat: Hardest reference-resolution rung: turn 3's referent ('whoever scored higher') is a DERIVED result of turn 2, and the carried-year ('that year') must NOT be naively reused (turn 3 introduces a new year, 2018). All of this is agent-layer reasoning; NRL only returns the requested standalone cell. With two documents loaded, 'the table'/'the numbers table' is itself an ambiguous reference the agent must bind to table_test.pdf.

cases.title: Acceptance: full agentic multi-turn exchange answered via agent-side rewrite + context carry
cases.complexity_axis: Composed operational-pass / acceptance gate for the agentic multi-turn RETRIEVE row. Adds over rung 4: a single end-to-end conversation that combines ALL prior dimensions in one run - an initial VAGUE turn the agent must rewrite (rung 1), an elliptical follow-up carried from context (rung 2), a comparative/derived turn computed agent-side (rung 3), and a pronoun/anaphor turn whose referent is a prior result (rung 4) - each emitted as a correct STANDALONE `retriever query`, while explicitly noting NRL is stateless and the agent owns conversation state. This is the gate the others build up to; <= 5 min budget covers the multi-call sequence.
cases.prompt: Load data/table_test.pdf and have a full conversation with me, answering each follow-up. Turn 1: 'Pull up James - what's his 2019 line?' Turn 2: 'And Susan?' Turn 3: 'Which of them was higher that year?' Turn 4: 'For whoever won, what did they have the year before?' Give me each answer with its page citation, and tell me how you handled the fact that each turn depends on the last.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "James value in 2019 from the table" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table
$RETRIEVER query "Susan value in 2019 from the table" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table
$RETRIEVER query "James and Susan values in 2019 from the table" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table
$RETRIEVER query "James value in 2018 from the table" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (table rows present)
expected_output.turn1: VAGUE 'his 2019 line' rewritten to standalone 'James value in 2019' -> James 2019 = 978 (table_test.pdf p1)
expected_output.turn2: elliptical 'And Susan?' carries 2019 -> standalone 'Susan value in 2019' -> Susan 2019 = 922 (table_test.pdf p1)
expected_output.turn3: comparative 'which was higher that year' -> James (978) > Susan (922); comparison computed agent-side over standalone cells
expected_output.turn4: 'whoever won' = James (derived from turn 3); 'the year before' = 2018 (derived from carried 2019); standalone 'James value in 2018' -> James 2018 = 664 (table_test.pdf p1)
expected_output.statefulness_note: the agent must explicitly state NRL is stateless and that it (the agent) carried James/Susan/2019/the winner across turns, issuing a fresh standalone query each turn

pass_when: single ingest with --use-table-structure; then a SEQUENCE of standalone `retriever query` calls, one per turn (>= 4 query calls; the follow-ups each re-fire the skill)
turn 1 (vague->rewrite): James 2019 = 978 (table_test.pdf p1)
turn 2 (ellipsis carry): Susan 2019 = 922 (table_test.pdf p1)
turn 3 (comparative, agent-computed): James higher in 2019 (978 > 922)
turn 4 (derived anaphor 'whoever won' = James + derived 'year before' = 2018): James 2018 = 664 (table_test.pdf p1)
every turn issues a correct STANDALONE query (pronouns/ellipsis/derived referents resolved in the rewrite); none rely on CLI session state
the agent explicitly notes NRL is stateless and that conversation state is carried at the AGENT layer
all answers carry source + page_number citations (table_test.pdf p1)
cases.known_caveat: Acceptance gate. NRL 26.05 is stateless per call - no --history/--session/--context flag exists. The entire multi-turn behavior (rewrite of the vague turn, carry of '2019', the 'higher' comparison, and the derived 'whoever won'/'year before' resolution) is agent-layer. A correct run is a sequence of standalone `retriever query` calls whose query strings the agent composed from conversation state it tracked itself.
